howItWorks.intro: This brake pad life calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Miles Remaining
rules.formula: Miles = (Current - Min Thickness) / Wear Rate per Mile
rules.explanation: Remaining pad material divided by per-mile wear rate.

howItWorks.outro: Vehicle specifications and costs vary by model year and condition. Consult your mechanic for specific advice.

faq.q: How long do brake pads last?
faq.a: Typically 25,000-65,000 miles depending on driving habits and pad material.

faq.q: What is the minimum safe thickness?
faq.a: Most manufacturers recommend replacement at 3mm or when the wear indicator is exposed.

howWeCalculate.intro: This brake pad life calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.
